Displays the number of working days that can be fully covered before a shortage occurs for the selected material, based on its current stock. Planned receipts are not taken into account:

 

As a production planner, you can use stock days' supply information to evaluate the current planning results for a selected material. Based on the current stock and the requirement information included in the stock / requirements list for a material, the system determines the day of the first shortage and displays the number of working days until the shortage occurs in this column:

  • For shortages that occur in the future, the system counts the number of working days that can be fully covered and displays it as a positive value. Neither the current date nor the day of the first shortage are taken into account for this calculation.

  • For shortages that occur today or in the past, the system counts the number of working days between the day of the first shortage and the current date and displays it as a negative value. The day of the first shortage and the current date are taken into account for this calculation, that is, a shortage that occurs today results in a stock days' supply value of -1.

The values displayed in this column can differ according to the shortage definition that you select.

The same dependencies apply also here with the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels app for the current date. 

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is below the safety DOS, the value in the Stock Days' Supply column is highlighted in red.

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is equal to the safety DOS or between the safety DOS and the target DOS, the value in the Stock Days' Supply column is highlighted in green.

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is equal to or above the target DOS, the value in the Stock Days' Supply column is highlighted in yellow.

Intraday Supply (Working Days) 

Displays the number of working days that can be fully covered before a shortage occurs for a material, plus two decimal places to indicate which fraction of the requirements on the day of the first shortage can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. The intraday supply is determined based on the current stock and the receipts and requirements information included in the stock / requirements list for a material.

As a production planner, you can use intraday supply information to evaluate the current planning results for a selected material. The value displayed in this column includes the following information:

  • Days' supply: the number of full working days until a shortage occurs

 

  • Fractional coverage of the requirements on the day of the first shortage: fraction of the requirements that can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. It is calculated by dividing the available quantity by the aggregated days' demand.

    The system determines the value for this column based on the following formula: Intraday Supply = Days’ Supply + Fractional Coverage on the Day of the First Shortage.

The value displayed in this column can differ according to the shortage definition that you select.

 

For materials with the values defined in time-dependent safety stock levels (MTDSL) for the current date, the system automatically compares the number of full working days until the first shortage (days' supply) with the Safety DOS and Target DOS values that are defined in the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels app for the current date. Based on the result of this comparison, it adapts the coloring of the intraday supply value as follows:

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage (days' supply) is below the safety DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in red.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to the safety DOS or between the safety DOS and the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in green.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to or above the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in yellow.

The coloring of the intraday supply value is determined based on full working days. The fractional coverage on the day of the first shortage is not taken into account. So, the coloring is the same as for Days Supply. 

Stock Intraday Supply (Working Days)

Displays the number of working days until a shortage occurs for a material, plus two decimal places to indicate which fraction of the requirements on the day of the first shortage can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. The stock intraday supply is determined based on the current stock and the requirements information included in the stock / requirements list for a material. Planned receipts are not taken into account.

 

As a production planner, you can use stock intraday supply information to evaluate the current planning results for a selected material. The value displayed in this column includes the following information:

  • Stock days' supply: the number of full working days until a shortage occurs, based on the current stock of a material

  • Fractional coverage of the requirements on the day of the first shortage: fraction of the requirements that can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. It is calculated by dividing the available quantity by the aggregated days' demand.

    The system determines the value for this column based on the following formula: Stock Intraday Supply = Stock Days’ Supply + Fractional Coverage on the Day of the First Shortage.

The value displayed in this column can differ according to the shortage definition that you select.

For materials which have values defined in the time-dependent safety stock levels, the system automatically compares the number of full working days until the first shortage (stock days' supply) with the Safety DOS and Target DOS values that are defined in the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels app for the current date. Based on the result of this comparison, it adapts the coloring of the stock intraday supply value as follows:

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage (stock days' supply) is below the safety DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in red.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to the safety DOS or between the safety DOS and the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in green.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to or above the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in yellow.

The coloring of the stock intraday supply value is determined based on full working days. The fractional coverage on the day of the first shortage is not taken into account. Also here the same principal as for Stock days supply.

entry.title: To RISE or Not To RISE
entry.content:
This is a key decision facing most organisations that run their SAP estate in an on-premise environment or natively on a hyperscaler and are thinking about the future.

It is not an easy decision to make and requires deep analysis relating to their current operating model, the resource and skill mix available and SAP’s future roadmap and strategy. Organizations also need to identify the expected operational, commercial and process benefits to justify the move to RISE and change their status quo.

Having been associated with RISE with SAP since its inception, I have seen how SAP’s offering has matured over the years. SAP is making significant investments into the operational and delivery capabilities of RISE. SAP’s strategy has also evolved to fully support this cloud model. It is my firm belief that NOW is the right time for customers to decide and move to RISE with SAP. Here are some of the reasons.

 

Maturity

RISE with SAP was launched by SAP’s CEO, Christian Klein in January 2021. Since then, the offering has matured significantly. The commercialisation of a large proportion of SAP’s  previously on-premise solution offerings has occurred to allow them to now be offered in the cloud model.

There has been significant investment in tools, automation and people so that the delivery of the cloud service is world class. The ‘Enterprise Cloud Services’ or ECS organisation is the delivery arm of SAP for the RISE offering where these investments have occurred.

ECS supports the operational delivery & support of the RISE offering and is separate to the implementation services which implements S/4HANA. ECS is purely involved in the operational, day-to-day, business-as-usual (BAU) maintenance and management of customer’s SAP landscape under RISE.

 

New Board Area - Customer Services & Delivery

At the start of 2024, a new board area was established called, ‘Customer Services & Delivery’ which brings together all the resources involved in the architecture and delivery of RISE and associated services. This board area now has architecture and pre-sales teams involved in architecting our customer’s cloud solutions (Cloud architects), the services teams that implement the S/4HANA capabilities (Services teams) and the teams involved in the actual day-to-day operational delivery of service for RISE customers (ECS team).

This was a strategic decision and is extremely important as it reflects the internal transformation within SAP as it expands from an engineering, product development, and a sales organisation, into a cloud service provider ensuring customer success through adoption and delivery of cloud services and focuses on achieving successful customer outcomes.

Our customers will gain from RISE with SAP by utilising SAP’s expertise and experience in managing S/4HANA customers globally and benefiting from SAP’s focus on customer adoption and outcome driven approach.

You can read about these here.

 

Incentives

Since RISE with SAP launched, SAP has been listening to customer feedback and has released the RISE with SAP Migration and Modernisation program in Jan 2024. The news article is here.

Through this program SAP recognises the complexity of the transformation required and offers to actively support customers on their journey to the cloud and to help make the transition easier.

The program offers customers a Migration Methodology to provide guidance across their migration journey, providing roadmaps for innovations, and more significantly, it offers commercial incentives for customers moving to RISE.

This incentive is called the RISE Transformation Incentive and is a limited time fund through till the end of 2024 that SAP has created to support customers moving to RISE. Through this program, SAP customers can leverage this fund and receive incentives in the form of credits as a means of offsetting some of the costs involved in their transformation journeys.

This incentive will be offered to current SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC on-premise customers moving their SAP landscape to RISE and they will stand to gain credits as a percentage of the amount of their RISE with SAP subscription. These credits can be utilized to offset either the on-premise maintenance costs, implementation services costs or the cost of the RISE subscription fee itself.

To date I have engaged with several customers that have benefited from this incentive. It is my view that this incentive fund represents a significant opportunity for customers to initiate their move to RISE in 2024.  

 

Partner Ecosystem

When RISE was first launched, our partner ecosystem was quite apprehensive. However, over time, our partners have also understood that RISE with SAP is at the core of the SAP Cloud Strategy and have now jointly worked with SAP to tailor their own offerings based on the RISE implementation methodologies.

Several of SAP’s partners have also become RISE customers including Accenture, IBM, Wipro, HCL, Microsoft, which further goes to show the strength of the offering and the trust that partners, most of whom have their own SAP practices, bestow upon SAP in managing their SAP landscapes for them.

 

So, if you are sitting on the fence and trying to decide whether the time is right to move to RISE, I would say that 2024 is definitely the year you should make the decision and take the plunge. I can safely say that you will get the kind of support from SAP that you would have never seen before!

entry.title: Why QR IBAN is automatically prefilled in the invoice
entry.content:
If the Supplier has only a QR IBAN defined in the master data (IBAN with interval 30000-31999 on positions 5-9), then the QR IBAN is automatically prefilled to the incoming invoice during the posting. The accountant must provide only a QR Reference (a unique 27chars reference that is required for a QR invoice).

This ensures that the incoming invoice is posted correctly and the payment will not be rejected later by the payment program due to missing QR data.

If the Supplier has other regular IBANs in the master data in addition to the QR IBAN, the QR IBAN is not filled in automatically and all other invoice types can be posted.

entry.title: Live Session focused on SAP S/4HANA Utilities - Innovations and Benefits (June 27, 2024, at 4:00 PM
entry.content:
Dear SAP consultants and customers interested in SAP S/4HANA Utilities,

We are excited to invite you to participate in a live session focused on SAP S/4HANA Utilities - Innovations and Benefits. This session will be held on June 27, 2024, at 16:00 GMT+2 and will last for approximately 1 hour.

During this session, you will have the opportunity to gain a general understanding of the SAP Utilities strategy and the relevance of SAP S/4HANA Utilities. Additionally, you will have the chance to deep dive into the innovations, including a system demo, and learn about the SAP S/4HANA Utilities Roadmap and sources of further information.

Whether you are a beginner, intermediate, or advanced in your knowledge of this topic, there will be valuable insights to gain from this live session. This event is open to both Business Users and Consultants, and we encourage anyone with an interest in SAP S/4HANA Utilities to participate.

For more information and to register for the event, please visit the following link:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/sap-s-4hana-utilities-innovations-and-benefits

We look forward to having you join us for this informative and engaging session.

Best regards,
Greg

Utilities SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Evaluate Consolidated Manual Classification of SAP Users to Discover SAP License Potential Savings
entry.content:
Do you manage multiple SAP systems and have a measurement plan for SAP audits but haven't proactively analyzed whether your manual classification of SAP users aligns with your SAP license entitlement? With the SAM4U solution from SAP, this task becomes effortless, helping you maintain your SAP landscape within entitled SAP license boundaries.

How it works?

Install the SAM4U extractor from SAP in your SAP backend systems, gather data with almost one click, and enjoy consolidated analysis done by the SAM4U Tool.

Where to get SAM4U extractor?

The SAM4U Extractor is deployed into the customer's backend systems to extract data. Its functionality has been adapted (downported) for compatibility with release 7.31 and is distributed via SAP Note:
3292019 - SAM4U Data Extractor - SAP for Me

Which SAM4U KPI shows consolidated results of manual classification of SAP users?

Once you have uploaded data to the SAM4U tool and created a consolidation according to your SAP measurement plan, as shown in the example below:

 Pic.01. SAM4U Consolidation

You can access all SAM4U KPIs that are automatically filled with data based on the extracted information from SAP backends. A demo instance of the SAM4U Tool is always available on our website: Skybuffer

On the Dashboard, as well as in the Users Analytics section, you can find the “Classified Users” KPI, which displays the number of classified users for each type.

 Pic.02. SAM4U Tool – Classified Users KPI

By clicking on the chart, you can drill down to the list of users.

 Pic.03. SAM4U Tool – List of Users Assigned to a Certain Metric

In our example, you can see that 402 SAP users are classified as “SAP Bus. Suite Professional,” which is the most expensive type of license. Checking the License Consumption KPI in the SAM4U tool, we see that the number of “SAP Bus. Suite Professional” licenses purchased is less than the number of SAP users manually classified as professionals.

Pic.04. SAM4U Tool – License Consumption

Optimizing Manual Classification of SAP Users

Considering the spare licenses of other SAP user types, several options exist for optimizing the manual classification of SAP users to comply with SAP license entitlement:

  • Evaluate Manual Classifications: Use the SAM4U KPI “Classified/Dialog Users” from user analytics to ensure all users are manually classified. Automatically classified users are typically assigned the most expensive license type, so reviewing and reclassifying these users is essential.

  • Identify Dormant Users: Check the SAM4U users analytics for dormant users who might be locked and invalidated.

  • Analyze User Roles and Usage: Use the SAM4U tool to identify users whose roles and active usage do not justify their current classification, allowing for potential reclassification.

SAM4U Tool brings clarity and helps you reclassify SAP users effectively, ensuring proper SAP license consumption and unlocking potential cost savings.

Get familiar with the SAM4U tool available on our website: Skybuffer and keep reading our blogs.

P.S. Check out our entire blog series about the SAM4U tool here: SAM4U as a Service.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter July 2024
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Please note: A one-time registration for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support [accessible here] is necessary to register for these events.


End-to-end Performance Optimization in SAP Analytics Cloud
The overall performance of SAP Analytics Cloud is mainly determined by the three main factors: Client time (front end0), Network time, and Service time (back-end). Discover how you can use SAP Enterprise Support services to focus on fine-tuning SAP back-end data sources.
Learn more


SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay Overview
Are you an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition user looking to enhance your knowledge of the procure-to-pay processes? Enroll our e-learning course to deep dive into the processes of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay.
Full details in this blog post

 


SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Are you ready to take your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation project to the next level? Join our e-learning to gain a comprehensive overview of SAP Cloud ALM for implementation and learn how it can help you manage your implementation project from start to finish.
More details


New Learning Program for SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront

Join our new learning program to get familiar with composable architecture principles. Learn how to use SAP Commerce Cloud for building and managing e-commerce storefronts, and design and customize storefronts using the storefront approach.
Get started

 

FURTHER NEWS AND UPCOMING SESSIONS

Introducing our New Integration Learning Program for Commerce Cloud

Find in the Customer Experience value map all details about how to integrate your Commerce Cloud solution with success.
More details in this blog

SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps is Your Gateway to Guided Enablement

Our structured learning content elevates expertise for efficient skill development in evolving technologies.
See this video for more details or contact the value map team

SAP CQC Deployment Readiness for SAP Customer Data Cloud: New Updates for B2B

CQC for Deployment Readiness for SAP CDC is a comprehensive service that analyzes your SAP Customer Experience tenant in detail to identify potential risks and ensure a smooth start of production. With the latest updates, we have expanded the scope of our checks to include B2B scenarios, providing you with even more assurance that your implementation will be successful.
See more detail in the blog or contact the CX Value map team

Register now for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series

Join the series of 20 live sessions coming up on July 8-12 that highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. Take the chance and register now.
Further information

See What’s New for the Release Navigator for SAP BTP in Q2 2024

Stay up to date with the latest releases for SAP BTP. Our new blog post highlights the updates and enhancements made to the Release Navigator in Q2 2024. Discover new product pages incl. SAP Build Code and foundational services like SAP Cloud Identity Services, SAP Automation Pilot and more.
Read the blog post and check out the navigator

SAP for Me Mobile App - Interact With SAP From Anywhere and at any Time

With the SAP for Me Mobile App you can easily interact with SAP from anywhere and at anytime. You have complete transparency into your SAP products all in one place. The app's focus is to streamline and simplify everyday tasks, making it ideal for handling straightforward processes and providing you with a simplified experience.
Get an overview on SAP Community: Interact with SAP anywhere, anytime with SAP for Me

Customer Support at SAP: Where Artificial Intelligence Meets Application Integration

Customer Support is a differentiating factor among companies that are equally positioned in functional richness, usability, quality, deployability, or configurability. While signing up a new customer is considered a huge win, being able to delight and retain the customer is equally important. With SAP’s focus on value realization, the push to enhance Customer Support with Artificial Intelligence and application integration has never been higher. Learn more about how SAP’s Customer Support is owning and driving end-to-end customer support experiences powered by AI.
Read more

Retaining Customers Through Support Experience and Efficiency

While the primary role of Customer Support is to fix customer issues promptly and effectively, it is equally important to address the needs of support engineers. This article explores how the customers’ support experience and the engineers’ support efficiency work together in delighting and retaining customers.
Link to the full article

Ask an Expert Peer: Looking for Experts

This program is designed to accelerate solutions for SAP customers by connecting them with a network of talented individuals. Experts can dedicate as many (or as few) hours, making it the perfect side gig opportunity. Although case volume may be a little slow initially, it's expected to ramp up quickly.
Click here to sign up today:

  • SAP SuccessFactors, SAP BTP and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Procurement

iValue: Navigating a journey to ERP in the cloud with the SAP Cloud ALM

iValue highlights the crucial role of the SAP support team and SAP Cloud ALM in their successful migration to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition. SAP Cloud ALM significantly improved efficiency by reducing project management reporting efforts by 75%.
Find more information
